skill.name: performance-vitals
skill.description: Enforce Core Web Vitals optimization. Use when building user-facing features, reviewing performance, or when Lighthouse scores drop. Covers LCP, FID/INP, CLS, and optimization techniques.
skill.summary: Guide frontend teams through Core Web Vitals optimization, Lighthouse checks, and performance budget workflows.
skill.author: Doyajin174

security_audit.summary: The static findings are mostly false positives from Markdown code fences, examples, and documentation links. No malicious intent, credential access, prompt injection, or data exfiltration was found, but the skill allows Bash and recommends Lighthouse commands that execute local tooling and perform network requests.

medium_findings.title: Bash Permission and Lighthouse Command Execution
medium_findings.description: Verdict: TRUE_POSITIVE for operational risk. The skill grants Bash access and recommends running npx lighthouse, which can execute installed or downloaded tooling. This appears legitimate for performance auditing, but users should review commands and target URLs before execution. Confidence: 0.78. Confidence reasoning: Bash permission and the command are directly present, but the context is a normal Lighthouse workflow rather than malicious code.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Bash permission and the command are directly present, but the context is a normal Lighthouse workflow rather than malicious code.

medium_findings.title: Network Activity in Performance Measurement Examples
medium_findings.description: Verdict: TRUE_POSITIVE for disclosure risk. The skill includes preconnect, Lighthouse URL scans, analytics metric sending, and external documentation links. These are expected for web performance work, but generated workflows may contact external services or send performance telemetry. Confidence: 0.72. Confidence reasoning: Network-related examples are explicit, but no credentials, secrets, or unauthorized destinations are present.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Network-related examples are explicit, but no credentials, secrets, or unauthorized destinations are present.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alert Is a False Positive
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The high static alert points to the description line, which mentions FID and INP performance metrics, not cryptographic algorithms. No evidence found of MD5, SHA-1, encryption, hashing, or security-sensitive cryptography. Confidence: 0.96. Confidence reasoning: The cited line is plain metadata for Core Web Vitals and contains no cryptographic code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line is plain metadata for Core Web Vitals and contains no cryptographic code.

low_findings.title: Markdown Code Fences Misread as Backtick Execution
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE for the repeated Ruby or shell backtick alerts. The cited regions are Markdown code fences containing TypeScript, TSX, CSS, JSON, YAML, and checklist examples. They are instructional snippets, not executable Ruby backtick calls. Confidence: 0.93. Confidence reasoning: The file is Markdown documentation, and the backticks delimit examples rather than execute commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file is Markdown documentation, and the backticks delimit examples rather than execute commands.

low_findings.title: Web Worker Example Is Instructional
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE for malware risk. The Worker construction appears in a performance example that moves heavy computation off the main thread. It loads a local worker module and does not fetch remote code. Confidence: 0.86. Confidence reasoning: The code creates a local worker for Core Web Vitals optimization, with no dynamic URL or remote script source.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code creates a local worker for Core Web Vitals optimization, with no dynamic URL or remote script source.

dangerous_patterns.title: Allowed Bash Tool
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill metadata allows Bash. This is useful for Lighthouse audits, but it permits command execution when an assistant follows the skill. Confidence: 0.82. Confidence reasoning: Bash is explicitly listed in allowed tools, and the workflow includes a command-line audit step.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Bash is explicitly listed in allowed tools, and the workflow includes a command-line audit step.

dangerous_patterns.title: External URL Scanning
dangerous_patterns.description: The Lighthouse examples audit URLs and can trigger outbound requests. The example uses placeholder domains, but users should avoid scanning private or unauthorized targets. Confidence: 0.74. Confidence reasoning: The network behavior is inherent to Lighthouse URL audits, but the cited examples use non-sensitive placeholder URLs.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The network behavior is inherent to Lighthouse URL audits, but the cited examples use non-sensitive placeholder URLs.

content.value_statement: Slow pages reduce conversions, search visibility, and user trust. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through LCP, INP, CLS, Lighthouse, and practical frontend fixes.
actual_capabilities: Reviews LCP, INP, and CLS targets against current Core Web Vitals thresholds.
Suggests image, font, preload, and critical CSS changes for faster LCP.
Recommends task splitting, React transitions, and Web Workers for better INP.
Identifies layout shift causes from images, fonts, dynamic content, and animations.
Provides Lighthouse CI and performance budget guidance for release workflows.
limitations: It does not run performance tests unless the assistant has tool access and permission.
It uses example URLs that must be replaced with real application URLs.
It cannot guarantee Search ranking improvements from performance changes alone.
It focuses on frontend web performance, not backend profiling or infrastructure tuning.

use_cases.title: Review a Pull Request
use_cases.description: Check a user-facing change for LCP, INP, and CLS regressions before merge.

use_cases.title: Set Performance Budgets
use_cases.description: Create Lighthouse CI budgets that block releases when vitals exceed agreed thresholds.

use_cases.title: Plan a Vitals Remediation Sprint
use_cases.description: Turn Lighthouse or field data issues into a prioritized fix list for product pages.

prompt_templates.title: Check Basic Vitals
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of one page or feature.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this page for Core Web Vitals risks. Focus on LCP, INP, and CLS. Give clear fixes I can apply first.

prompt_templates.title: Improve a Slow Hero
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this hero section for LCP problems. Check image loading, font loading, preloads, and render blocking resources.

prompt_templates.title: Reduce Interaction Delay
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced INP analysis for interactive screens.
prompt_templates.prompt: Find likely INP issues in this component. Suggest task splitting, handler changes, React scheduling, or Web Worker use.

prompt_templates.title: Create a CI Performance Gate
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a Lighthouse CI plan for these routes. Include budgets, monitored metrics, failure thresholds, and rollout advice.

output_examples.input: Review a landing page with a large hero image and custom fonts.
output_examples.output: The likely LCP target is the hero image. Use a sized optimized image, preload the asset, set font display swap, and remove render blocking CSS.

output_examples.input: Analyze a search page that feels slow while typing.
output_examples.output: The main INP risk is heavy work inside the input handler. Defer search updates, split validation work, and move expensive processing off the main thread.

output_examples.input: Build a performance budget for checkout and dashboard pages.
output_examples.output: Track LCP, INP, CLS, JavaScript size, and total blocking time. Fail builds only after baseline budgets are agreed and noisy routes are stabilized.

best_practices: Use real field data when available, then confirm fixes with Lighthouse and browser performance traces.
Review generated commands before running them, especially commands that scan URLs or install tooling.
Prioritize the largest user impact first, then add CI budgets to prevent regressions.
anti_patterns: Do not optimize only for a single Lighthouse score while ignoring real user measurements.
Do not add analytics events that send personal data with performance metrics.
Do not preload every asset, because excessive preloads can delay more important resources.

faq.question: What metrics does this skill focus on?
faq.answer: It focuses on LCP, INP, and CLS, with supporting guidance for Lighthouse and performance budgets.

faq.question: Can it run Lighthouse automatically?
faq.answer: Only if the assistant has tool access and you approve the command and target URL.

faq.question: Does it support Next.js projects?
faq.answer: Yes. Many examples use Next.js Image, Next.js font loading, and React scheduling patterns.

faq.question: Does it replace a performance engineer?
faq.answer: No. It provides structured guidance, but complex regressions still need profiling and field data review.

faq.question: Is it safe for private applications?
faq.answer: Use care with commands and URLs. Avoid sending private pages or telemetry to external services without approval.

faq.question: Can it help with CI workflows?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes Lighthouse CI and budget guidance for release checks.
